titleStmt.title: Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 12

div.p: Robert, by the grace of God humble servant of the church of St Andrews, to the sons of holy mother Church everywhere, Greeting: Let it be known both to those to come as well as to those present that we have given, and granted in free alms to God and to the church of St Andrews and to the canons serving God there, Kinninmonth with the toft of Kilrymont and with all its other appurtenances. Wherefore we will and enjoin that they shall hold this alms as freely and honourably as alms ought to be held. And if anyone should bring injury to the church of St Andews and to the aforesaid brethren, let him be bound with the bond of anathema, unless he makes proper satisfaction. As witnesses: Herbert the bishop of Glasgow, William the bishop of Moray, Arnold the abbot of Kelso, Geoffrey the abbot of Dunfermline, William the abbot of Holyrood, Isaac the abbot of Stirling, Matthew the archdeacon, Thor the archdeacon, Aiulf the dean, Nicholas the king's clerk, William the bishop's chaplain, Master Andrew, Master Herbert, John the bishop's nephew, Ralph the bishop's nephew.
